home.heroDescription: You know you need AI. Together we find where to start, what creates the most value, and launch the first useful solution.
home.painTitle: AI only makes sense when it solves a real business problem.
home.pain1Title: You need a clear first step
home.pain1Description: There are many options. Before choosing a tool, we name the business problem worth solving first.
home.pain2Description: AI agents should not become another vague experiment. We define upfront how they save time, reduce cost, improve quality, or create customer value.
home.pain3Description: Data access, rules, permissions, and rollout matter. Your team needs AI systems they can use confidently without putting company data at risk.
home.pain3AtlasText: See the public Agent Threat Atlas
home.consultingDescription: If you know AI matters but do not know where to start, I map opportunities, pick the first project worth doing, and define how success will be measured.
home.implementationDescription: If you have a clear problem, I design and build a practical AI workflow or agent that fits your process, tools, and safety constraints.
home.trainingDescription: If the solution needs to stick, I train your team to use it confidently, understand its boundaries, and improve the workflow over time.

processSteps.title: Discovery Call
processSteps.description: 30 minutes, free. We clarify where you are, what you want to improve, and whether AI is the right next step.

processSteps.title: Your Roadmap
processSteps.description: I propose a clear plan with the first use case, success metric, safety rules, and rollout path.

processSteps.title: Execution
processSteps.description: We build the approved workflow, test it on real tasks, train the first users, and measure whether it saves time, reduces cost, improves quality, or creates customer value.

processSteps.title: Handoff
processSteps.description: Once it works, your team takes ownership. Long-term support stays available if you want it.

firstStepOffer.title: AI opportunity and risk audit
firstStepOffer.description: In 1–2 weeks we map where AI makes sense in your company, which workflow to tackle first, what risks need controls, and how success will be measured.

home.atlasProofTitle: AI agent security is not an abstract risk.
home.atlasProofDescription: I maintain a public atlas of agentic AI incidents, mapping real failures to threat categories and prevention principles teams can use before rollout.
home.trustTitle: Built on practical value, not hype
home.trustDescription: I recommend the technology and rollout path that fit your business problem, team, budget, and safety needs.
home.ctaBandTitle: Ready to find your first useful AI project?
home.ctaBandDescription: Most clients start with a 30-minute consultation. No commitment, no jargon.

services.heroDescription: From first AI use case to safe rollout.

consulting.description: I assess your current workflows, identify the highest-impact AI opportunities, and build a concrete adoption roadmap. You get clarity on what is worth automating, what should wait, and how value will be measured.

RAG and knowledge workflows where useful
Evals, monitoring, and safe rollout support

notSure.title: Not sure where to start?
notSure.description: Most clients start with a 30-minute consultation. No commitment, no jargon — just an honest conversation about where AI might create measurable value.

about.heroDescription: One person, one mission: making AI work for real teams.
about.bioText:
I help small and medium-sized businesses turn AI urgency into practical, safe, measurable AI agent workflows.

My work starts with a real business problem, not a tool. Together we identify the right first workflow, define how value will be measured, and build a safe path from experiment to production.

I combine 10+ years of software engineering experience with hands-on work in LLM systems, RAG, evals, LLMOps, automation, and agentic workflows.
about.principle1Description: Open-source or managed, self-hosted or cloud — I recommend what fits your team, budget, and compliance needs.
about.principle2Description: I train your team alongside deployment. The goal is for you to own and evolve your AI systems independently.
about.principle3Description: No 6-month projects. I deploy fast, measure results, and expand only what works.
about.techDescription: The right setup depends on the workflow. Sometimes that means a lightweight automation, sometimes a RAG system, sometimes a custom AI agent connected to your existing tools. The common thread is clear rules, controlled access, measurable outcomes, and a team that knows how to use it.
about.atlasText: I ground safety decisions in real incidents, threat categories, and controls, not vague caution.
about.ctaTitle: Let's talk about the first AI workflow worth solving.

contact.heroDescription: Whether you have a clear project or just questions — I'm here.
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell me about your project or question...
contact.success: Message sent! I'll get back to you within 24 hours.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again or email me directly.
contact.bookDescription: Pick a time that works. 30 minutes, no strings attached.

faq.question: We do not know where to start with AI. Is it worth getting in touch?
faq.answer: Yes. This is the most common starting point. On the first call we look at your processes, data, and the places where the team loses time. The goal is a clear first use case, not a list of tools.

faq.question: How long does the first rollout take?
faq.answer: Most first projects move from kickoff to a working workflow in 2–6 weeks. A simple automation can be live in days. More complex systems take longer, but you should see progress every week.

faq.question: How do we know whether AI is creating real value?
faq.answer: Before building, we define the metric: time saved, shorter cycle time, fewer errors, better quality, or faster customer response. If value cannot be measured, we narrow the project or postpone it.

faq.question: How do you handle data safety?
faq.answer: We start with data, permissions, and rollout boundaries. The first version should use the minimum access needed, clear rules for sensitive data, and human review where risk is higher. We move slower where safety matters.

faq.question: Do we need our own technical team?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. I can handle the technical side. Someone technical on your team helps with long-term maintenance and ownership, but the first project can start without an internal developer.

faq.question: What tools do you work with?
faq.answer: The tool depends on the problem, data, and safety requirements. For workflow automation I use n8n, CrewAI, or LangChain/LangGraph. For models I choose between frontier models such as OpenAI and Anthropic, or open models such as Mistral and Llama. For closed-source agentic coding tools, Codex and Claude Code can make sense. For more controlled or self-hosted agents, OpenClaw or Hermes Agent may fit. The goal is not to use more tools, but to choose a stack that is safe, maintainable, and creates measurable value.

faq.question: What happens after handoff?
faq.answer: Once the workflow works, your team gets the operating rules, training, and enough context to use it safely. The goal is capability, not dependency. If useful, we can keep improving the workflow together.

footer.description: Agentic AI for small and medium-sized businesses that want safe, measurable results.
